notes: Gemini CLI has no built-in autonomous self-prompting mode (no /goal, no Stop-hook re-prompt, no max-iterations cap; YOLO mode only auto-approves tools and nudges the model, it does not re-prompt across turns). The loop is driven EXTERNALLY: the driver feeds successive prompts to one live `gemini -y` REPL session, each advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal (count 1->2->3). The spec asserts what the irrlicht autonomous-loop primitive actually cares about for a turn-delimited adapter: each iteration cycles ready->working->ready cleanly, the session never sticks in working and never flips to waiting (yolo auto-approves every tool -> no permission gates), and the loop ends in ready. This mirrors the codex sibling (also externally driven) and relaxes claudecode's strict 'stays continuously working' span the same way (claudecode's /goal also flickers ready<->working per Stop-hook iteration). gemini-cli emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is INFERRED from a final text-only 'gemini' message with non-empty content and no open tool calls (parser.go ~L209) - so each iter_done is emission-heuristic. ANCHORING (corrected against the live recording 2026-06-12-10-55-48): the proc-<PID> presession and the rollout session race at birth - in this recording the rollout session was born BEFORE the proc-<PID> presession, so a two-tier presession_birth->session_birth(new_session) pin mis-resolved session_birth to the presession. session_birth therefore anchors UNPINNED to start (first ready) and the iteration phases time-chain off it WITHOUT same_session_as - only the rollout session ever goes working, so the working->ready arcs unambiguously belong to it. NO teardown phase: the daemon emits NO process_exited for gemini-cli in coexist mode (proc-PID presession only ever gets transcript_removed, never the rollout) - verified absent across every gemini-cli recording incl. this one (0 process_exited events) - so asserting an exit edge would assert an event the daemon does not emit. observations.model = gemini-3.5-flash (the API-key-tier default boot model the recording captured); cost+tokens nonzero.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md -> Feature: Autonomous loop (self-prompting) - modeled as an externally-driven loop; cf. codex/claudecode siblings

text: Rollout session reconciles and is born ready (anchor UNPINNED to start so the proc-<PID> presession birth-order race can't steal the anchor)
Loop start: first externally-fed prompt -> ready -> working on the rollout session (only the rollout ever goes working)
First iteration completes -> working -> ready (state cycles cleanly between iterations, not stuck working)
Next loop iteration: driver re-prompts -> ready -> working - loop is active
Second iteration completes -> back to ready
Final loop iteration: driver re-prompts -> ready -> working
Loop terminates: final iteration ends in ready; the session never entered waiting across the whole loop (gemini -y auto-approves every tool, no permission gates)
